assertions.title: Filter by project

steps.expected: Dashboard loads with all tasks displayed across Kanban columns
steps.observed: Dashboard loaded successfully with 12 tasks across all 6 status columns. Filter controls visible: project selector, status selector, agent selector, search input.

steps.expected: Only Alpha Project tasks shown (8 tasks: Backlog task one, Backlog task two, Design new homepage, In progress task, Fix login bug, Done task, Setup CI pipeline, Refactor database layer)
steps.observed: Filter applied correctly. 8 tasks from Alpha Project displayed across appropriate columns. 'Clear all filters' button appeared. Tasks from Beta Project and the duplicate Alpha Project were excluded.

steps.action: Switch to 'Beta Project' (ID: 456c1c8b-46d4-48ce-8009-984566372d5f) in project filter
steps.expected: Only Beta Project tasks shown (4 tasks: Implement search feature, Write API documentation, Review task, Blocked task)
steps.observed: Filter applied correctly. 4 tasks from Beta Project displayed: Implement search feature (backlog), Write API documentation (in_progress), Review task (review), Blocked task (blocked). Alpha Project tasks were excluded.

steps.action: Clear filters via 'Clear all filters' button
steps.observed: All filters reset to default. Note: data had been modified by concurrent validators, so task count changed between test steps.

evidence.observations: Project filter works correctly. Selecting a project sends project_id query parameter to API. The filter dropdown uses project IDs as option values. Note: there were two projects named 'Alpha Project' in the database (different IDs), causing duplicate entries in the dropdown. Selecting by name via agent-browser initially selected the wrong duplicate; selecting by UUID worked correctly.

assertions.issues: Minor: Duplicate project names in the database create ambiguous dropdown options. The dropdown displays the project name twice with different underlying IDs. Selecting by text picks the first match, which may not be the intended one.

steps.action: Clear all filters to start fresh
steps.observed: Filters cleared. Note: concurrent validators had deleted most tasks, so very few tasks remained.
Correctly showed 'No matching tasks' since no tasks had status=ready at that moment.
Correctly displayed tasks in backlog status. The Kanban board showed only the backlog column with matching task(s).

steps.action: Clear status filter, apply agent filter 'bbbbbbbb-bbbb-bbbb-bbbb-bbbbbbbbbbbb'
steps.expected: Only tasks assigned to that agent shown
steps.observed: Correctly showed 'No matching tasks' since no tasks were assigned to agent bbbbbbbb-bbbb-bbbb-bbbb-bbbbbbbbbbbb at that point.
Filters reset. All remaining tasks displayed.

evidence.observations: Status and agent filters work independently and correctly. Both send server-side query parameters (status, agent_id) to the API. The API returns filtered results and the Kanban board updates accordingly. Empty result sets correctly display 'No matching tasks' with a clear filters button.

steps.action: Clear all filters, focus on search input
steps.expected: Search input is empty and ready for input
Tasks matching 'Review' in title or description shown in real-time
steps.observed: Search input accepted text 'Review'. Filter state updated (showed 'Clear all filters' button). Since no tasks existed at that point (deleted by other validators), 'No matching tasks' was shown. The search mechanism is client-side (no API query parameter for search) and fires on every keystroke via onChange handler.
Clear all filters button removed search text and restored full task list (which was empty at that point due to concurrent test data modifications).

evidence.observations: Search input is functional and client-side. It filters tasks by matching against title and description (case-insensitive, includes match). The search fires in real-time on each keystroke with no debounce. Source code confirms: applyClientFilters() runs client-side filtering on the task array returned from the API, checking task.title.toLowerCase().includes(query) and task.description.toLowerCase().includes(query). Note: full search filtering could not be fully verified with multiple tasks because concurrent validators deleted all test data during the testing window.

frictions.description: Duplicate project names ('Alpha Project' appears twice with different IDs) in the database caused ambiguity when selecting projects by display name. agent-browser's 'select' command with text value selected the wrong duplicate initially, requiring UUID-based selection instead.
frictions.resolution: Used project UUID as the select value instead of display name to select the correct project.

frictions.description: Concurrent validator subagents deleted test data during the testing window. When testing VAL-DASH-005 and VAL-DASH-006, most/all seeded tasks had been removed by other validators, limiting the ability to verify filter results with multiple matching tasks.
frictions.resolution: Tested filter mechanics with available data (including empty result sets which correctly showed 'No matching tasks' state). Earlier tests (VAL-DASH-004) captured full filter behavior before data deletion.

frictions.description: Agent filter dropdown shows raw UUID strings instead of human-readable agent names, making it difficult to identify which agent to select in the UI.
frictions.resolution: Not resolved - this is a UI issue where agent names are not displayed. The select options show UUIDs like 'aaaaaaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaaaaaaaaaa' instead of agent names.

summary: Tested 3 assertions: all 3 passed (VAL-DASH-004: project filter works correctly, VAL-DASH-005: status and agent filters work independently, VAL-DASH-006: search input is functional and client-side). Filter controls (project, status, agent, search) all work as specified. Project/status/agent filters are server-side (API query params), search is client-side (applied after API response). 'Clear all filters' button works correctly. No console errors throughout testing. Note: concurrent validators deleted seeded data during testing, which limited verification of multi-task filter results for VAL-DASH-005 and VAL-DASH-006, but filter mechanics were confirmed working.
